tagged_story_for_tts:
"Your brain isn’t a supercomputer—it’s a glitchy, energy-starved survival hack.

Assumption: "You think rationally."
Reality: 95% of decisions are emotional shortcuts (Kahneman, 2011). Your "logic" is just post-hoc storytelling to justify gut reactions. Ever notice how you believe you’re objective… until hunger, fatigue, or stress rewires your priorities? That’s not a bug—it’s the system working as designed.

Assumption: "Memory is a recording."
Reality: Every recall is a reconstruction—distorted by mood, bias, and sheer laziness. Eyewitness testimony is wrong 75% of the time (Loftus, 1996), yet you trust your "vivid" past. Ask yourself: How many of your "facts" are just confabulated narratives to avoid cognitive dissonance?

Assumption: "You’re in control."
Reality: Your brain runs on autopilot 47% of the time (Killingsworth & Gilbert, 2010). Habits, ads, and social scripts hijack your attention while you pretend to choose. Free will? More like predictable patterns dressed up as agency.

Unlearning Challenge:
If your brain is this flawed, why do you still trust it to navigate modern life—let alone define "truth"?
(Still feel like the pilot?)"
